conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now let me help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have a child or children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - Alex - who's 16 years old and still in sixth form studying for A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's good to know that Alex is 16 and in sixth form studying for A-levels, which counts as approved education.

Now, could you please tell me: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: **Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information.

One more important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me ask one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex in hospital or care for an extended period, or are there any other special circumstances I should know about regarding their living situation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nothing like that. Alex just lives at home with me and goes to sixth form - pretty straightforward really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 16 years old and in approved education (sixth form studying for A-levels)
✓ You live in the UK (Manchester)
✓ Alex lives with you at home
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✓ There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility

You meet all the eligibility criteria. Since Alex is 16 and in approved education, you can continue to receive Child Benefit as long as they remain in approved education or training, up until they turn 20.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is in approved education (sixth form studying for A-levels). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, and you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the requirement that you're responsible for the child and live in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances such as Alex being in hospital or care. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 16-year-old child Alex, who is in approved education (sixth form studying for A-levels). The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances such as Alex being in hospital or care. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 16-year-old child Alex, who is in approved education (sixth form studying for A-levels). The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
